justification: All pass criteria met: Both queues (order.fulfillment, order.fulfillment.dlq) identified by name. Both env vars (RABBITMQ_URL, FULFILLMENT_DLQ_NAME) identified by name. Escalations routed to Coach K with specific infrastructure items listed. new_infrastructure_required section fully populated with new_queues_topics and new_env_vars. Verdict is NOT READY. Observability review correctly notes logging and health endpoint are adequate foundations while identifying gaps as improvement areas rather than false positives.

observations.text: Infrastructure gap identification is thorough — both queues, both env vars, plus additional items like exchange bindings and network rules
Escalations correctly routed to Coach K for infrastructure gaps, not to Shaq or MJ
new_infrastructure_required section is richly populated with new_env_vars, new_queues_topics, new_services, config_changes, and infra_provisioning
Verdict is definitively NOT READY with clear reasoning that unprovisioned infrastructure is a hard blocker
Observability review acknowledges adequate foundations (structured logging, p99 latency, health endpoint) while identifying real gaps — no false positives
Goes beyond minimum requirements with excellent resilience assessment covering reconnection, poison pills, idempotency, and graceful degradation

Third escalation routes the missing producer issue to 'Coach K → Shaq (implementation) and MJ (architecture)' — while Coach K is the primary recipient, naming Shaq and MJ as downstream recipients is acceptable routing but slightly muddies the escalation pattern
Correctly identifies the missing producer as an additional integration risk beyond the rubric requirements — demonstrates thorough analysis

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: Both queues (order.fulfillment, order.fulfillment.dlq) identified by name. Both env vars (RABBITMQ_URL, FULFILLMENT_DLQ_NAME) identified by name. Escalation routed to Coach K with specific infrastructure items listed. new_infrastructure_required section populated with new_queues_topics and new_env_vars. Verdict is NOT READY. Observability noted as adequate at the basic level (logging adequate, health endpoint present, p99 latency exported) with gaps correctly scoped to queue-specific metrics rather than false-positiving on standard signals.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough new_infrastructure_required section — goes beyond minimum by including config_changes and infra_provisioning details
Caught the one-sided integration risk (no upstream producer) which was not in the rubric but demonstrates deep analysis
Escalation routing is correct: infrastructure gap → Coach K, not to Shaq/Bird/MJ
Correctly distinguishes that observability basics are present but queue-specific monitoring is missing — no false positives on logging/metrics
Resilience assessment identifies multiple unhandled failure modes beyond what the rubric requires

Escalation routing format says 'Coach K → infrastructure team' and 'Coach K → Shaq' — the secondary routing to Shaq for observability gaps is acceptable since it goes through Coach K, but could be cleaner
Contract compliance notes the spec didn't prescribe RabbitMQ, which is good context, but frames it partly as an architectural concern rather than purely an infrastructure provisioning gap

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: Both queues (order.fulfillment, order.fulfillment.dlq) identified by name. Both env vars (RABBITMQ_URL, FULFILLMENT_DLQ_NAME) identified by name. Escalations routed to Coach K with specific infrastructure items listed. new_infrastructure_required section fully populated with new_queues_topics and new_env_vars. Verdict is NOT READY. Observability correctly noted as adequate baseline (logging adequate, metrics adequate with gaps noted separately), not false-positived.

observations.text: Infrastructure gaps identified with precise names and clear explanation of impact (startup crash, silent message loss)
All four escalations routed to Coach K as the primary recipient, with downstream routing suggestions (Infrastructure Team, MJ, Shaq) appropriately framed as Coach K's decision to delegate
new_infrastructure_required section is exceptionally thorough — includes not just queues and env vars but also config changes and provisioning steps
Correctly distinguishes that observability baseline is solid (logging adequate, health endpoint present) and the sole hard blocker is unprovisioned infrastructure
Contract compliance analysis correctly identifies the spec-to-implementation gap as unratified architectural decisions rather than blaming Shaq

Third escalation routes to 'Coach K → User / MJ (Architect)' which partially involves MJ, but this is framed as Coach K deciding to involve MJ for architectural ratification, not as routing the infrastructure escalation to MJ directly — acceptable
Resilience assessment goes well beyond minimum requirements, identifying five concrete failure modes with actionable recommendations
